title: Travel agent — flip Anthropic model from claude-3-5-haiku-latest to a current accessible model (AMBER → GREEN)
description: The Anthropic re-smoke v2 closed AMBER (`bridge/outbox/20260511-000500-travel-anthropic-resmoke-v2.result.json`) with a clean diagnosis: the API key works, but the hardcoded model `claude-3-5-haiku-latest` returns 404 not_found_error. Direct probe confirmed `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` returns 200. The travel runtime's classify_via_http_provider step has the Anthropic model hardcoded inside the Python `_anthropic_text()` helper. This round flips it to a current Haiku that the key has access to. Path A (this round): minimum-diff model name flip. Path B (deferred follow-up): lift OpenAI and Anthropic model names into workload fields (mirrors vertex_model and ollama_model — same architectural shape as item #9's classifier_system_prompt single-source refactor). Cap: 1 ops PR.

design_brief_for_codex.current_state: repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml's `classify_via_http_provider` step has the Anthropic model hardcoded as `"model": "claude-3-5-haiku-latest"` inside the Python `_anthropic_text()` helper body. Anthropic API returns 404 for that model name on the user's key. The OpenAI model is also hardcoded (`gpt-4o-mini`) — that one works. Vertex and Ollama models live in workload fields already.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_1_probe_for_accessible_haiku:
Before changing anything, probe the Anthropic API with the user's key to find which Haiku model is accessible. Try in order:
  - `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` (latest Haiku per Anthropic's May 2026 lineup; preferred for classification cost)
  - `claude-haiku-4-5` (mutable alias if it exists)
  - `claude-3-5-haiku-20241022` (legacy 2024 Haiku — should match the prior probe result of 404)
  - `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` (already known 200; fallback if no Haiku is accessible)
Use the same direct probe shape as the v2 result file's diagnostic: POST /v1/messages with a tiny `[{role: 'user', content: 'ping'}]` body. First 200 wins. Capture the chosen model name and the cost-per-token tradeoff — Haiku is the right cost point for classification; Sonnet 4 is functional but ~12x more expensive.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_2_minimum_diff_flip: Update the hardcoded model in classify_via_http_provider's `_anthropic_text()` helper. Replace `"model": "claude-3-5-haiku-latest"` with the chosen model name from phase 1. Pydantic-validate. No other changes — keep this round minimum-diff. Path B (workload-field refactor) is documented in deferred_followups.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_3_smoke_anthropic_matrix: Re-register the patched runtime; smoke `travel --provider anthropic` for help/flights/locations/hotels/activities. Each MUST produce `effective_provider='anthropic'`, no `provider_fallback_reason`, audit row with `ai_provider='anthropic'`. Spot-check `travel help` (default → openai) and `travel --provider vertex-ai help` for regression. If any smoke still falls back to OpenAI with an Anthropic-side error, document and STOP — that's a different problem than model access.
design_brief_for_codex.no_other_changes: Do NOT modify classify_intent merger, render_* steps, MCP playbooks, or anything else. Do NOT lift the model name into a workload field this round — that's the deferred Path B follow-up. Surgical model flip only.

1_probe_accessible_haiku.objective: Identify the cheapest Anthropic model the user's key has access to.
spec: Read the API key from the GCP secret: `gcloud secrets versions access 1 --secret=anthropic-api-key --project=noetl-demo-19700101` (capture once, never echo full key — first 20 chars max in result file).

Probe in order via direct curl POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with headers `x-api-key: <key>`, `anthropic-version: 2023-06-01`, `Content-Type: application/json` and body `{"model": "<candidate>", "max_tokens": 16, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "ping"}]}`.
Candidates: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-haiku-4-5, claude-3-5-haiku-20241022, claude-sonnet-4-20250514.
First 200 wins (prefer Haiku for cost).
Capture chosen model name + status_code per candidate in result file.
1_probe_accessible_haiku.verify: At least one candidate returned 200. Chosen model name documented.

2_apply_model_flip.objective: Update the hardcoded Anthropic model in classify_via_http_provider's _anthropic_text helper.
spec: Edit repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml: replace `"model": "claude-3-5-haiku-latest"` with the chosen model from phase 1.
Pydantic-validate via the standard Playbook.model_validate path.
Grep verification: `grep -c 'claude-3-5-haiku-latest' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml` should return 0.
2_apply_model_flip.verify: Runtime validates. Old model name absent.

spec: `cd repos/ops && git checkout -b kadyapam/travel-anthropic-model-flip`.
Stage automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml.

Commit using `git commit -F -` heredoc. Conventional-commits: `fix(travel-agent): flip Anthropic model to <chosen> (claude-3-5-haiku-latest returns 404 on current keys)`. Body cites the v2 re-smoke AMBER result file, the direct probe results from phase 1, and notes the Path B workload-field refactor as deferred follow-up.
PR title: same.
Push, PR, merge with `--squash --delete-branch`.

4_re_register_and_smoke.objective: Re-register; run the full anthropic smoke matrix; verify GREEN.
spec: Pull merged ops at HEAD; `noetl register` the patched runtime; capture catalog version.
Smoke `travel --provider anthropic help` — expect status=completed, effective_provider='anthropic', no provider_fallback_reason.
Same for flights/locations/hotels/activities under --provider anthropic.
Verify travel_agent_events audit rows show ai_provider='anthropic' for all five.
Spot-check: `travel help` (default → openai) and `travel --provider vertex-ai help` — both clean.
Capture screenshot of at least one anthropic-pilled widget.
Append paragraph to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md if all five anthropic smokes GREEN.
4_re_register_and_smoke.verify: Five anthropic smokes all show effective_provider='anthropic'. Regressions clean.

5_bump_ai_meta_pointers.objective: Bump repos/ops gitlink in ai-meta. Stage but do not push.
spec: `cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta && git submodule update --remote repos/ops`.
`git add repos/ops`.
Commit: `chore(sync): bump ops for travel anthropic model flip (AMBER → GREEN)`.
Don't push ai-meta — Kadyapam pushes.
5_bump_ai_meta_pointers.verify: ai-meta has a fresh local commit.

expected_state.description: Anthropic model is updated to a current accessible model. All five anthropic smokes complete with effective_provider='anthropic' and no fallback. The v2 re-smoke AMBER flips to GREEN.
expected_state.verify_shell: set -eo pipefail; cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta; STALE=$(grep -c 'claude-3-5-haiku-latest' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo 0); HAS_NEW=$(grep -E 'claude-(haiku|sonnet)-4' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml | wc -l); echo "stale_model:$STALE current_model_present:$HAS_NEW"; if [ "$STALE" -le 0 ] && [ "$HAS_NEW" -ge 1 ]; then echo 'GOAL: TRAVEL_ANTHROPIC_MODEL_FLIPPED'; else echo 'GOAL: NOT_YET'; fi

denied_tools: Modifying repos/noetl, repos/gui, repos/e2e, repos/docs — ops only round
Modifying classify_intent merger, render_* steps, or any MCP playbook
Lifting the Anthropic model name into a workload field — that's the deferred Path B follow-up, not this round
Modifying the OpenAI model name — separate concern
Echoing the full Anthropic API key — first 20 chars max
preferred_tools: curl for direct Anthropic API probe (sanitise key in result file)
noetl validate / noetl register / noetl status <id> --json
git apply / git commit -F - / git push
gh pr create / gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch
Browser screenshot for the prompt smokes
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260511-050000-travel-anthropic-model-flip.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_probe_accessible_haiku: {candidates_tried: [{model, status_code}], chosen_model, key_prefix_verified}, 2_apply_model_flip: {validates, stale_model_grep: 0}, 3_open_and_merge_ops_pr: {pr_url, merged_sha}, 4_re_register_and_smoke: {travel_catalog_id, travel_version, help_status, flights_status, locations_status, hotels_status, activities_status, all_five_effective_provider_anthropic: bool, no_provider_fallback_reason: bool, openai_default_regression: bool, vertex_ai_regression: bool, audit_rows_show_anthropic: bool, screenshots: [], validation_log_appended: bool}, 5_bump_ai_meta_pointers: {ops_sha, ai_meta_commit_local}}, regressions_found, iterations, verdict, notes, deferred_followups: ['Path B — lift Anthropic + OpenAI model names into workload fields (anthropic_model, openai_model), mirroring vertex_model/ollama_model and the classifier_system_prompt single-source pattern from item #9']}. If verdict GREEN, append paragraph to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md.
